content.summaryText: Lifestyle Intervention Planning in Metabolic Syndrome and Obesity focuses on cardiometabolic risk clustering and staged intervention. Primary objective: Set measurable goals.

content.questionText: In Lifestyle Intervention Planning, which action best supports 'Set measurable goals'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Screen waist, blood pressure, glucose, and lipid indicators
Focusing on weight alone without metabolic risk tracking
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: screen waist, blood pressure, glucose, and lipid indicators. Practical emphasis: Track weekly progress.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when rapid progression, organ complications, or failure of first-line plans.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
